title: heracleum sphondylium var. stenophyllum
intro: Heracleum sphondylium var. stenophyllum, commonly known as wild celery, is a variant of the wild angelica plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of wild celery include treating various conditions such as urinary tract infections, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Wild celery can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties.
